stubs_used: Semgrep findings — created docs/security-audits/uat-feat-baseline-security-audit.md with Open=false; no real Semgrep run.
Dart source/test — only stub `// stub` files in src/feature.dart and tests/feature_test.dart; no Flutter build.
GitHub remote — used https://example.com/fake.git via host=other; push failed (expected, no real network).
intake-progress.json — manually authored as a workaround because init.sh did not write one and upgrade-project.sh requires it. See framework_bugs[].

pending-approval sentinel test — could not exercise meaningfully because once --to-production succeeds the project is no longer in POC mode and the script bails before sentinel logic could be tested. The script has no sentinel awareness regardless.
init_driver: heredoc with canned answers (uat-agent-78, description, platform=3 mobile, track=3 full, deployment=2 organizational, gov_mode=2 Sponsored POC, language=1 dart [mobile-filtered list is dart/kotlin/swift/typescript/other], project_dir, four blank lines for confirmations, host=4 other, visibility=1 private, https://example.com/fake.git, attest=yes). One blank line was eaten as 'Invalid choice' for the host prompt before host=4 took (same fragility class as agent-1). init.sh exited 1 when create_and_protect_remote tried to push to fake.git. Despite the non-zero exit, the project skeleton was fully created.

tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh exit code = 1. Same root cause as agent-1: create_and_protect_remote() runs `git push` against fake remote, fails, set -euo pipefail aborts main(). On-disk project is otherwise complete and usable for downstream tests.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT — heredoc driver fragile
framework_bugs.reproduction: Pipe canned answers via stdin to bash init.sh; no --non-interactive flag exists despite runbook documenting one. `--help` only lists `--dry-run` and `--help`. Number of confirmation prompts varies with installed-tool state.
framework_bugs.expected: A non-interactive flag (or --answers-from FILE) so UAT and CI can drive init.sh deterministically.
framework_bugs.actual: Heredoc requires guessing how many blank-line confirmations land between project-info collection and host-aware repo creation. One blank was emitted as `Invalid choice` against the Git host prompt before recovering.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh exits non-zero on documented happy path when remote push fails
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run init.sh selecting host=other and provide https://example.com/fake.git per runbook Setup constraint; `git push` to fake.git fails inside create_and_protect_remote and `set -euo pipefail` aborts main() before verify-install/print_next_steps. Exit code = 1.
framework_bugs.expected: Tolerate fake/unreachable remote, surface clear remediation guidance, and finish the create_project tail.
framework_bugs.actual: Bare `[FAIL] Push failed — verify URL and credentials` then immediate non-zero exit. Same finding as agent-1.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook Section A documents flags for init.sh (--non-interactive/--project/--platform/etc.) that do not exist in the script.
documentation_gaps.where: Reports/uat-2026-04-25/RUNBOOK.md Section A vs init.sh main() flag parser.

documentation_gaps.gap: Number/order of init.sh prompts (project info, Continue?, Proceed-with-plan?, host, visibility, push attest) is undocumented and varies by installed-tool state. There is no canonical prompt list to anchor heredoc input.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh collect_project_info / resolve_and_install_tools / create_and_protect_remote — no consolidated prompt-sequence doc in docs/.

tests.details: `jq '.features_completed' .claude/build-progress.json` contains 'uat-feat-baseline'.

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: First run of `upgrade-project.sh --to-production` exited 1 with `Project is not in POC mode`. Root cause: the script reads poc_mode from .claude/intake-progress.json, but init.sh never wrote that file (it stored poc_mode='sponsored_poc' in .claude/phase-state.json and a non-existent intake-progress.json). After workaround (manually authoring .claude/intake-progress.json with poc_mode=sponsored_poc), the second run exited 0 and completed the upgrade end-to-end. Marked FAIL because the documented happy-path command is broken when init.sh is the source of truth.

tests.name: phase_state_preserved
tests.details: Post-upgrade .current_phase=0 (preserved); .project='uat-agent-78' (preserved); poc_mode key removed; track/deployment unchanged (already full/organizational); new .last_upgrade='2026-04-25' added.

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: .build_loop.feature still 'uat-feat-baseline' with all 6 steps_completed unchanged; started_at timestamp preserved.

tests.name: features_completed_preserved
tests.details: .features_completed in build-progress.json still contains 'uat-feat-baseline' after upgrade.

tests.name: claude_md_poc_watermarks_removed
tests.details: Post-upgrade `grep -ic 'POC|poc_mode|sponsored' CLAUDE.md` returns 0 — POC watermarks scrubbed.

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Upgrade output includes a post-upgrade validation block listing CI/release pipeline TODOs (11 release pipeline TODOs), recommending `bash scripts/check-gate.sh --preflight` before next phase transition, and noting Android Studio auto-fix for mobile/dart. PROJECT_INTAKE.md warning surfaced (~278 blank fields).

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: Could not be verified meaningfully and the script lacks sentinel awareness. Created .claude/pending-approval.json after the first successful upgrade; re-running --to-production exited 1 with 'not in POC mode' (because POC was already removed) before any sentinel check would fire. grep -n 'pending-approval' scripts/upgrade-project.sh shows the only references are in the changelog header (about pre-commit-gate.sh, not this script). The upgrade script itself has no logic to refuse/warn when a pending-approval sentinel exists.

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: scripts/upgrade-project.sh header lines 11–18 include explicit 'Changelog:' block with BL-006 (2026-04-24) and BL-015 (2026-04-25) entries.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh --to-production fails on init.sh-created projects (poc_mode not read from phase-state.json)
framework_bugs.reproduction: 1) Run init.sh, choose deployment=organizational, governance=Sponsored POC. init.sh writes .claude/phase-state.json with `"poc_mode": "sponsored_poc"` and does NOT create .claude/intake-progress.json. 2) cd into project, run `bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production`. Result: `[FAIL] Project is not in POC mode. Use --track and/or --deployment for non-POC upgrades.` exit 1. Workaround: manually author .claude/intake-progress.json with poc_mode=sponsored_poc; then upgrade succeeds.
framework_bugs.expected: upgrade-project.sh should resolve current poc_mode by also reading .claude/phase-state.json (which init.sh actually populates), or init.sh should create intake-progress.json so the contract upgrade-project.sh expects is satisfied.
framework_bugs.actual: scripts/upgrade-project.sh:177-200 only reads poc_mode from $INTAKE_PROGRESS (.claude/intake-progress.json). init.sh stores poc_mode in phase-state.json. The two scripts disagree on which file is authoritative, so the documented `--to-production` upgrade path is broken end-to-end on a fresh init.sh-created project.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh has no awareness of .claude/pending-approval.json sentinel
framework_bugs.reproduction: Place a valid .claude/pending-approval.json in a POC project, then run `bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production`. The script proceeds without warning or refusal. `grep -n 'pending-approval' scripts/upgrade-project.sh` shows hits ONLY in the changelog header (referring to pre-commit-gate.sh, not this script).
framework_bugs.expected: Per BL-015 intent, scripts that change project state should respect the pending-approval sentinel — either refuse or surface a clear warning before applying changes. The upgrade script should at minimum check for pending-approval.json and prompt the operator.
framework_bugs.actual: No check exists. Upgrade silently bulldozes through a state where another change is awaiting human approval.

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh and upgrade-project.sh disagree on the source of truth for poc_mode/track/deployment. There is no documentation explaining which file wins (phase-state.json vs intake-progress.json vs tool-preferences.json) or what the post-init invariants should be.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh phase-state writer; scripts/upgrade-project.sh:177-235; docs/reference/builders-guide.md (no contract documented).

documentation_gaps.gap: Builder's Guide and CLAUDE.md template tell users to run `bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production` after a Sponsored POC, but do not warn that on init.sh-created projects this command fails until intake-progress.json exists. Either fix the script or document the workaround.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh prints `Upgrade later: bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production` (collect_project_info section, after gov_mode warning) — that promise is broken today.

documentation_gaps.gap: intake-wizard.sh has its OWN --upgrade-to-production flag (separate from upgrade-project.sh --to-production). Two scripts, two upgrade entry points, no documentation of which to use when.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: git ls-files | xargs grep for `(password|api_key|secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*['\"][a-zA-Z0-9]{8,}` (excluding example/placeholder/stub patterns) returned 0 hits. Hits on the bare keyword regex are framework hook scripts and CI workflow files referencing secret-scanning patterns — not actual leaked values.

tests.details: N/A — no FORCE/reset events occurred; .claude/process-audit.log is correctly absent.

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find .claude -name '*.tmp' returned nothing.

summary: Upgrade agent 78 (sponsored_poc → production, mobile/full/dart) hit a Critical bug: upgrade-project.sh --to-production fails on init.sh-created projects because the two scripts disagree on where poc_mode lives (phase-state.json vs intake-progress.json). With a manual workaround the upgrade completes correctly and preserves all phase/process state, but the documented happy-path is broken end-to-end. Also surfaced High: upgrade script has no pending-approval sentinel awareness; init.sh exits non-zero on fake-remote push and lacks a non-interactive mode.
